info.title: Perplexity Sonar API
info.description: Perplexity Sonar API — LLM with built-in web search. Returns AI-generated answers with citations from web sources.

model.description: Selects which Sonar model handles the request, trading off speed, depth, and cost. Should match the model implied by the endpoint path.

messages.description: Ordered conversation history sent to the model; the last user message is the query to answer, while earlier turns provide context.

role.description: Identifies who authored the message: `system` sets behavior, `user` is the asker, `assistant` is a prior model reply.

content.description: The text body of this message turn.

max_tokens.description: Upper bound on tokens generated in the answer; the model stops once this limit is reached. Leave unset to use the model default.

temperature.description: Sampling randomness: lower values yield more focused, deterministic answers; higher values increase diversity.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling: the model only samples from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches this threshold. Usually tune either this or temperature, not both.

top_k.description: Restricts sampling to the K highest-probability tokens at each step; a value of 0 disables this filter.

stream.description: When enabled, the answer is pushed incrementally as server-sent events instead of returned in a single response.

search_context.description: How much web search context the model pulls in before answering; more context can improve thoroughness but raises per-request cost.

frequency_penalty.description: Discourages reusing tokens in proportion to how often they already appeared, reducing verbatim repetition.

presence_penalty.description: Discourages tokens that have appeared at all, nudging the model toward introducing new topics.

return_citations.description: Whether the response should include the source citations and search results backing the answer.

search_recency_filter.description: Restricts web search to results published within the given recency window (e.g. past hour/day/week/month), useful for time-sensitive queries.

search_domain_filter.description: Whitelist of domains the web search is confined to; only sources from these domains are considered.

id.description: Unique identifier of this completion, useful for logging and tracing.

model.description: The Sonar model that actually produced this completion.

object.description: Type tag of the returned object, marking it as a chat completion result.

created.description: When the completion was generated, as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

index.description: Position of this candidate within the choices list.

role.description: Author role of the reply, which is the assistant.

content.description: The AI-generated answer text, containing inline citation markers such as [1][2] that map to the citations list.

message.description: The reply message produced by the model for this candidate.

finish_reason.description: Why generation stopped: `stop` for a natural end, `length` when the token limit was hit.

choices.description: The generated answer candidates; typically contains a single entry holding the model's reply.

citations.description: Source URLs cited in the answer, indexed to match the inline [n] markers in the answer text.

search_results.description: The web search results the answer was grounded in, each with metadata about the source.

title.description: Title of the search result page.

url.description: Link to the search result source.

snippet.description: Excerpt of the source text relevant to the query.

date.description:
Publication date of the source.
[⚠️Note:源码未声明日期格式，待研发确认。]

source.description: Name of the publisher or site the result came from.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the input messages.

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the generated answer.

total_tokens.description: Sum of prompt and completion tokens for the request.

search_context_size.description: The effective amount of search context applied, reflecting the search_context setting used.

usage.description: Token consumption and search usage accounting for this request.

post.description: Run a single-turn chat completion against the lightweight `sonar` model, which performs a live web search and returns an AI-written answer with source citations. Optimized for quick, low-cost factual lookups.

messages.content: What are the latest developments in quantum computing?

200.description: Successful response with AI answer and citations

post.summary: Sonar Pro — advanced search for complex queries
post.description: Run a chat completion against the `sonar-pro` model, which handles more complex, multi-step queries and follow-up reasoning while grounding the answer in live web search and citations.

messages.content: Compare the economic policies of the US and EU regarding AI regulation in 2025-2026

200.description: Successful response with AI answer and citations

post.summary: Sonar Reasoning Pro — chain-of-thought reasoning with search
post.description: Run a chat completion against the `sonar-reasoning-pro` model, which applies chain-of-thought reasoning over live web search results for step-by-step analytical answers.

messages.content: Analyze whether Tesla stock is overvalued based on current P/E ratio compared to industry averages

200.description: Successful response with AI answer and citations

post.summary: Sonar Deep Research — exhaustive research & comprehensive reports
post.description: Run a chat completion against the `sonar-deep-research` model, which performs exhaustive multi-step web research and produces a comprehensive report. Requests can take noticeably longer than other Sonar models.

messages.content: Write a comprehensive analysis of the global semiconductor supply chain risks in 2026

200.description: Successful response with AI answer and citations
